commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh Go and cry in the ears of Jerusalem, saying, Thus says the LORD; I remember you, the kindness of your youth, the love of your betrothal, when you followed me in the wilderness, in a land that was not sown. He speaks ironically. (Theodoret) Yet the Israelites at first showed greater proofs of love than they did afterwards. It is true they often prevaricated, in the wilderness, Exodus xxxii. , and Amos v. , and Psalm lxxvii. (Calmet) The Lord declares his gratuitous love, and then upbraids his people. (Haydock) He had caused them to multiply in Egypt, and gratuitously made choice of them. (Worthington).

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
